title: ptelea pinnata
intro: Ptelea pinnata, also known as the prickly ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Ptelea pinnata include relief from digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The herbal preparations of Ptelea pinnata include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by drying the bark, leaves, and roots of the plant.
